data.content:
Link Analysis
A topic likely related to web search or network analysis, scheduled for Week 3 of the course.<SEP>A course topic focused on analyzing links.

Learning to Rank
A topic related to machine learning applied to ranking problems, scheduled for Week 5 of the course.<SEP>A course topic on methods for learning to rank.

Vector Space Model
A topic likely related to information retrieval or data representation, scheduled for Week 2 of the course.<SEP>A course topic covering the Vector Space Model.

Recommender Systems (Content-Based; Collaborative Filtering)
A course topic on recommender systems, including content-based and collaborative filtering approaches.

Word2vec
A course topic covering the word2vec model.

Evaluation of Search Engines
A topic focusing on assessing the performance of search technologies, scheduled for Week 4 of the course.<SEP>A course topic dedicated to evaluating the performance of search engines.

BM25
A topic likely referring to an information retrieval ranking function, scheduled for Week 2 of the course.<SEP>A course topic covering the BM25 ranking function.

Recommender Systems (Matrix Factorization; Bayesian Personalized Ranking)
A course topic on recommender systems, including matrix factorization and Bayesian personalized ranking.

Neural Ranking
A course topic focused on neural ranking methods.

Transformer
A course topic covering the Transformer architecture.

BERT-Based Ranking
A course topic focused on BERT-based ranking methods.

Neural Collaborative Filtering
A course topic on neural collaborative filtering.

Sequential Recommendation
A course topic covering sequential recommendation methods.

Large Language Models Basics
A course topic providing foundational knowledge of large language models.

A course topic exploring the integration of large language models with search engines.

AI
The concept of Artificial Intelligence, related to an academic statement.

Course
The academic course to which all assignments, policies, and schedules mentioned in the text pertain.

First Assignment
An introductory assignment designed to familiarize students with Jupyter Notebook and common Python operations.

Jupyter Notebook
A tool introduced in the first programming assignment to help students get familiar with Python operations.<SEP>A software tool used in the course for which the first assignment provides familiarization.

Python Operations
Common operations in Python used in the course, covered by the first assignment.

Quizzes
Four brief in-class quizzes administered during the course.

Quizzes Grade Component
This component contributes 20% to the final course grade and is comprised of the Quizzes.

Final Exam Grade Component
This component contributes 30% to the final course grade and is based on the comprehensive Final Exam.

Mar 7
The due date for the Group Project Proposal.

Final Exam Schedule
The schedule for the final exam, set from 3:30 PM - 5:30 PM on May 4.

HRBB 113
The meeting location for CSCE 670.<SEP>The designated location for the comprehensive final exam.

Homework
General assignments that are individual in nature and subject to the Homework Collaboration Policy.<SEP>A specific type of assignment that is subject to the Homework Late Day Policy and due by 11:59 PM on the due date.

Homework Collaboration Policy
An academic policy that outlines the rules for collaboration on homework assignments, emphasizing individual work while allowing discussion of approaches.

Group Project Grade Component
This component contributes 20% to the final course grade and is based on the Group Project.

Prohibited Collaboration Methods
Specific actions such as pair programming, sharing code, or working through specific problems in code together, which are not allowed under the Homework Collaboration Policy.

Regrade Policy
An academic policy detailing the procedure for requesting a regrade within seven days of receiving a graded item, requiring a written explanation.<SEP>This policy outlines the process and conditions for students to request a regrade for an assignment, requiring a written request within seven days and allowing the instructor the right to regrade the entire assignment.

Graded Item
Any assignment or exam that has been graded and returned to students, subject to the Regrade Policy.

Instructor
The person responsible for the course who must receive written explanations for regrade requests.<SEP>The instructor is responsible for reviewing written regrade requests and has the right to regrade an entire assignment.

3:30 PM - 5:30 PM
The specific time slot for the comprehensive final exam.

Acknowledging Help
A rule under the Homework Collaboration Policy requiring students to acknowledge all assistance received in their homework submissions.

Seven Days
The period within which a regrade request must be submitted after receiving a graded assignment.

Written Explanation
A mandatory written document detailing the reasons for a regrade request, to be submitted to the instructor.

CSCE 670
CSCE 670 is a course named "Information Storage and Retrieval" offered in Spring 2026.

Information Storage and Retrieval (CSCE 670)
This is the full title of the CSCE 670 course.

Spring 2026
Spring 2026 is the academic term during which CSCE 670 is offered.

CSCE 670 Syllabus
This document serves as the official course syllabus for CSCE 670.

CSCE 670 Section 600
Section 600 of CSCE 670, identified by the CRN 46627.

CSCE 670 Meeting Days
CSCE 670 meets on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays.

College Station
The location where CSCE 670 is offered.

Lecture (CSCE 670)
The meeting type for CSCE 670 is Lecture.

CSCE 670 Start Time
CSCE 670 begins at 1:50 PM.

CSCE 670 End Time
CSCE 670 concludes at 2:40 PM.

CSCE 670 Start Date
CSCE 670 begins on January 12, 2026.

CSCE 670 End Date
CSCE 670 ends on May 5, 2026.

CSCE 670 Credit Hours
CSCE 670 is a 3-credit hour course.

Yu Zhang's Email
The email address for instructor Yu Zhang is yuzhang@tamu.edu.

PETR 222
The office location for instructor Yu Zhang.

Yu Zhang
Yu Zhang is the instructor for CSCE 670.

Yu Zhang's Phone Number
The phone number for instructor Yu Zhang is (217) 954-3457.

Yu Zhang's Office Hours
Yu Zhang holds office hours on Mondays from 3:30 PM to 4:30 PM.

Information Representation (CSCE 670)
A topic within CSCE 670 focusing on representation of multimedia document collections.

Information Storage (CSCE 670)
A topic within CSCE 670 focusing on storage of very large multimedia document collections.

Information Access (CSCE 670)
A topic within CSCE 670 focusing on access to very large multimedia document collections.

data.entity_name: Data Structures and Algorithms for Information Retrieval (CSCE 670)
data.content:
Data Structures and Algorithms for Information Retrieval (CSCE 670)
A topic within CSCE 670 covering fundamental data structures and algorithms for information storage and retrieval systems.

Retrieval System Evaluation (CSCE 670)
A topic within CSCE 670 covering techniques to evaluate complete information retrieval systems.

Retrieval System Design (CSCE 670)
A topic within CSCE 670 covering techniques to design complete information retrieval systems.

Indexing Algorithms (CSCE 670)
A topic within CSCE 670 covering algorithms for indexing very large collections.

Querying Algorithms (CSCE 670)
A topic within CSCE 670 covering algorithms for querying very large collections.

Compressing Algorithms (CSCE 670)
A topic within CSCE 670 covering algorithms for compressing very large collections.

CSCE Department
The department responsible for offering courses such as CSCE 670 and CSCE 310.

CSCE 310
CSCE 310 is a prerequisite course required for enrollment in CSCE 670.<SEP>A prerequisite course required for enrollment in the Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course.

46627
The Course Reference Number (CRN) that uniquely identifies CSCE 670 Section 600.

(3-0) Course Structure (CSCE 670)
Describes the credit distribution for CSCE 670 as 3 hours of lecture and 0 hours of lab.

A scheduled event indicating a general due date on March 7.

AI Policy
A policy allowing the submission of AI-generated or AI-derived code, provided it is properly documented and adheres to specific standards of responsibility, attribution, and validation.

AI Statement Document
A document that outlines the academic policy regarding the use of AI in submissions.

AI-Generated Code
Code that has been created or significantly influenced by Artificial Intelligence tools.

Prompt
The input text or query provided to an AI tool, which must be included in the documentation when using AI-generated code.

Documentation Requirements
The stipulations for properly documenting the use of AI-generated code, including the prompt and significant parts of the response, in comments.

Response
The output generated by an AI tool in response to a prompt, significant parts of which must be included in the documentation when using AI-generated code.

Student Responsibility
The student's obligation to identify errors in program logic through comprehensive, documented testing, even when using AI-generated code.

Attribution Standards
Criteria that submissions must meet regarding the acknowledgment of sources or contributors.

Validation Standards
Criteria that submissions must meet to confirm their correctness, quality, or adherence to requirements.

Syntax Errors
Errors related to the grammatical structure of code, which AI tools may help avoid.

Program Logic Errors
Errors in the underlying reasoning or flow of a program, which students are responsible for identifying.

Comprehensive, Documented Testing
A method used to identify errors in program logic, requiring thorough and recorded tests.

AI Tools
Software or systems based on artificial intelligence that can assist in code generation and error avoidance.

Code Improvement Areas
Aspects for enhancing AI-generated code, specifically regarding separation of concerns and avoiding repetitions.

Separation Of Concerns
A principle of design for improving code, specifically mentioned as an area for improvement in AI-generated code.

Submission
Work submitted by students that must adhere to attribution and validation standards.

Avoiding Repetitions
A principle of design for improving code, specifically mentioned as an area for improvement in AI-generated code.

Assignment
A piece of academic work, such as a homework assignment, that can be subject to a regrade request or late work policies.

Late Work Policy
This general policy defines rules for submitting work after its due date, explicitly stating that makeup work for an excused absence is exempt from its provisions.

Makeup Work
Work submitted by a student to compensate for an excused absence, which is specifically exempt from the standard late work policy.

Student Rule 7
A specific academic rule referenced as the basis for exempting makeup work for excused absences from the late work policy.

Late Day
An indivisible 24-hour unit that students can use to extend homework deadlines, with a total allowance of five per student under the Homework Late Day Policy.

Homework Late Day Policy
This course-specific policy applies to all homeworks, dictating a due time of 11:59 PM, granting students five total late days (each an indivisible 24-hour unit), and specifying a grade of 0 for late assignments submitted after all late days are used.

Excused Absence
An absence that, when officially recognized (per Student Rule 7), allows a student to submit makeup work without it being considered late.

Course Schedule
A rough and subject-to-change outline of topics to be covered weekly throughout the course.

Boolean Retrieval
A topic related to information retrieval, scheduled for Week 1 of the course.

Course Overview
An introductory topic scheduled for Week 1 of the course.

TF-IDF
A topic likely related to text processing or information retrieval, scheduled for Week 2 of the course.

Recommender Systems
A topic covering methods for suggesting items, including Content-Based and Collaborative Filtering approaches, scheduled for Week 6 of the course.

Content-Based Recommender Systems
A specific approach within Recommender Systems, part of the Week 6 topics.

Week 1
The first week of the course schedule, covering Course Overview and Boolean Retrieval.

Collaborative Filtering Recommender Systems
A specific approach within Recommender Systems, part of the Week 6 topics.

Week 2
The second week of the course schedule, covering TF-IDF, Vector Space Model, and BM25.

Mining of Massive Datasets
An optional textbook authored by Jure Leskovec, Anand Rajaraman, and Jeffrey D. Ullman, published in 2014, and available online.<SEP>An optional textbook for the Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course.

Neural Models of Retrieval and Recommendation
This topic covers neural models used for retrieval and recommendation.

Jure Leskovec

Anand Rajaraman
An author of the textbook "Mining of Massive Datasets".

Christopher D. Manning
An author of the textbook "Introduction to Information Retrieval".

Jeffrey D. Ullman
An author of the textbook "Mining of Massive Datasets".

MMDS Website
The online resource for the "Mining of Massive Datasets" textbook, located at http://www.mmds.org/.

Introduction to Information Retrieval
An optional textbook authored by Christopher D. Manning, Prabhakar Raghavan, and Hinrich Schutze, published in 2009, and available online.

Prabhakar Raghavan
An author of the textbook "Introduction to Information Retrieval".

Hinrich Schutze
An author of the textbook "Introduction to Information Retrieval".

Course Grading Policy
This policy outlines the grading scale for the course, including percentages for different letter grades and components like Homework and Quizzes.

IR Book Website
The online resource for the "Introduction to Information Retrieval" textbook, located at https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/.

Grade Scale B
The grade 'B' is awarded for scores greater than or equal to 80% and less than 90%.

Grade Scale A
The grade 'A' is awarded for scores greater than or equal to 90%.

Grade Scale C
The grade 'C' is awarded for scores greater than or equal to 70% and less than 80%.

Grade Scale F
The grade 'F' is awarded for scores less than 60%.

Grade Scale D
The grade 'D' is awarded for scores greater than or equal to 60% and less than 70%.

Homework Component
This component accounts for 30% of the overall course grade and consists of five programming assignments.

Programming Assignments
Five programming assignments that contribute to the Homework Component, each to be completed in Python.

Python
The programming language used for all five programming assignments and operations in the course.

First Programming Assignment
An introductory programming assignment designed to familiarize students with Jupyter Notebook and common Python operations for the course.

Quizzes Component
This component accounts for 20% of the overall course grade and consists of four brief in-class quizzes.

Programming Assignment 1
An introductory programming assignment, contributing 2% to the overall course grade, designed to familiarize students with Jupyter Notebook and common Python operations for the course.

Programming Assignment 2
One of five programming assignments, contributing 7% to the overall course grade, to be completed in Python.

In-Class Quizzes
Four brief in-class quizzes that contribute to the Quizzes Component.

Programming Assignment 3
One of five programming assignments, contributing 7% to the overall course grade, to be completed in Python.

Programming Assignment 4
One of five programming assignments, contributing 7% to the overall course grade, to be completed in Python.

data.entity_name: Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course
data.content:
Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course
A course covering data structures and algorithms of information storage and retrieval systems, including techniques to design and evaluate complete retrieval systems.

Programming Assignment 5
One of five programming assignments, contributing 7% to the overall course grade, to be completed in Python.

CSCE 603
A prerequisite course required for enrollment in the Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course.

Graduate Classification
A required academic classification for enrollment in the Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course.

Approval of Instructor
An alternative prerequisite condition for enrollment in the Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course.

data.entity_name: Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course Web Search Concepts
data.content:
Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course Web Search Concepts
Upon completion of the Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course, learners will be able to define and explain key concepts and models relevant to web search.

data.entity_name: Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course Recommender Systems Concepts
data.content:
Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course Recommender Systems Concepts
Upon completion of the Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course, learners will be able to define and explain key concepts and models relevant to recommender systems.

data.entity_name: Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course Algorithm Design and Evaluation
data.content:
Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course Algorithm Design and Evaluation
Upon completion of the Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course, learners will be able to design, implement, and evaluate core algorithms underlying web search and recommendation systems.

data.entity_name: Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course Research Application
data.content:
Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course Research Application
Upon completion of the Information Storage and Retrieval Systems Course, learners will be able to identify salient features and apply recent research results in web search and recommender systems.
